rows.label: If the status is Unlocked, it means there are no crash events stored in the ECU memory. If the status is Locked, it indicates that crash events have been recorded. These can be cleared by following the steps. First, go to Live Data > Airbag Crashes Count Report category, and check the Crash reset counter value. This value must be greater than 0. Once you confirm it’s not 0, you can change the value of this setting to Unlocked. Please note, on some vehicle models, this setting is factory-locked and cannot be unlocked manually.

rows.label: Note that this works only if your vehicle is equipped with electric memory seats. To enable this feature, follow these steps: First, press one of the memory buttons (1, 2, or 3), and then select the driver mirror button. Next, put your vehicle in reverse gear (R) and adjust the position of the driver’s mirror for reverse. Then, hold the memory button for 5 seconds. After that, turn off the driver mirror button, shift to parking gear (P), and hold the memory button again for 5 seconds. To use this setting, select the driver mirror button and then shift to reverse gear (R).

After going back to Drive (D), the mirror will return to its original position after speeding up over 15 km/h (9 mph).
